Four Gen Z activists, Herizo Andriamanantena, Miora Rakotomalala, Dina Randrianarisoa and Nomena Ratsihorimanana, were arrested on 12 April, one of their lawyers said, two days after taking part in a protest calling for an election date to be set.

events.title: Trump extends US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely at request of Pakistan
events.summary:
US president says on Truth Social attacks are on hold until Iran submits proposal and talks reach end

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email

Donald Trump announced in a social media post on Tuesday that he was indefinitely extending a ceasefire with Iran at the request of Pakistan, which has been mediating talks, until the country responded to the United States’ negotiating positions or until talks reached a dead end.

“I have therefore directed our Military to continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able, and will therefore extend the Ceasefire until such time as their proposal is submitted, and discussions are concluded, one way or the other,” the US president wrote on Truth Social.

Continue reading...

events.title: Two Israeli soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue in Lebanon village
events.summary:
Israel Defense Forces say the ‘soldiers’ conduct completely deviated from IDF orders and value’

Two Israeli soldiers have been removed from combat duty and sentenced to 30 days in jail after one used a sledgehammer to smash a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon while the other filmed him, the Israel Defense Forces have said.

An image circulating on social media on Monday showed an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen from its cross in a Christian village in southern Lebanon, near the border with Israel, prompting outrage among Christian communities worldwide.

Continue reading...

events.title: Rabbi who boasts of bulldozing Palestinian homes will light torch for Israel’s national  day
events.summary:
Human rights campaigners say honour for Avraham Zarbiv endorses ethnic cleansing and war crimes

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

An extremist rabbi known for razing civilian homes in Gaza will light a torch at Israel’s independence day celebration on Tuesday, a role human rights campaigners said marked the embrace of genocide as the official “spirit of the nation”.

Avraham Zarbiv is one of 14 people chosen for their “extraordinary contribution to society and the state”, alongside a scientist, a Michelin-starred chef, a leading doctor, members of the security forces and entrepreneurs.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pakistan seeks to raise its global standing in push for Middle East peace
events.summary:
Islamabad has seized chance to act as mediator in Iran war and hopes to reap diplomatic and economic benefits

As Pakistan works frantically to narrow differences between Iran and the US in its newfound role as global peacemaker, it is also seeking to recast its diplomatic standing and attract business.

Pakistani officials, mediating between an unpredictable US president and hardliners in Tehran, were on Monday trying to coax both sides to put the conditions in place for a second round of talks in Islamabad this week, including easing the standoff in the strait of Hormuz. Pakistan was optimistic that the meeting would happen, viewing objections voiced by the Iranian side and Donald Trump’s threats as posturing for domestic audiences.

Continue reading...

events.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
events.summary:
Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights

Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists.

The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021, when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. Artillery fire, the burning of townships and arbitrary arrests have become common in the years since then.

Continue reading...

events.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
events.summary:
Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map

The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map.

It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament.

Continue reading...

events.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea
events.summary:
Trawler set off from Bangladesh and reportedly capsized due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding

About 250 people are missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the UN’s refugee and migration agencies.

The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. It had departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was bound for Malaysia.

Continue reading...

events.title: Indian music legend Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
events.summary:
Two-time Grammy nominee was one of Bollywood’s most versatile and celebrated voices

The Indian singer Asha Bhosle, whose voice defined Bollywood cinema and whose career spanned almost eight decades, has died in Mumbai at the age of 92.

Bhosle, who recorded more than 12,000 songs, became her country’s pre-eminent exponent of playback singing – recording tracks that were then lip-synced on film by actors. She also boldly embraced cabaret and western-influenced melodies to forge a distinctive musical identity.

Continue reading...

events.title: Olly Robbins’ account of Mandelson vetting piles pressure on Keir Starmer
events.summary:
Sacked civil servant tells select committee of ‘pressure’ to give clearance and ‘dismissive’ attitude to vetting

The civil servant sacked by Keir Starmer has given a devastating account of his government, saying Downing Street put huge pressure on the civil service to approve the appointment of Peter Mandelson as Washington ambassador despite the concerns of vetting officials.

Olly Robbins, the former top official at the Foreign Office, said No 10 took a “dismissive” attitude to vetting, and Mandelson was given access to the Foreign Office building and to “higher-classification briefings” before he was granted security clearance.

Continue reading...

events.title: Victims of sexual violence distressed by MPs’ ‘pugnacious’ questioning
events.summary:
Exclusive: Victims’ commissioner makes formal complaint after committee session left one attender ‘shocked, upset and extremely distressed’

Victims of rape and sexual violence have told parliamentarians they felt anxious and distressed during a Westminster evidence session, with one stating that witnessing “pugnacious” questioning had resulted in her “breaking down, sobbing and struggling to breathe”.

The victims’ commissioner has made a formal complaint to the chair of an influential group of MPs after a highly charged evidence session carried out by the public bill committee for the courts and tribunals bill about controversial changes to jury trials.

Continue reading...

events.title: Bill banning people born after 2008 from buying tobacco clears UK parliament
events.summary:
Ministers hope tobacco and vapes bill, which will become law next week, will create a ‘smoke-free generation’

A bill banning anyone born after 2008 from buying tobacco in the UK has completed its progress through parliament in a move that ministers hope will create a “smoke-free generation”.

Under the tobacco and vapes bill anyone born on or after 1 January 2009 will never be able to be legally sold tobacco across the UK, in an effort to save lives and reduce the burden on the NHS.

Continue reading...

events.title: Prolific unregulated sperm donor loses UK legal fight to be named as child’s father
events.summary:
Robert Albon cannot be declared four-year-old’s father because he ran illegal sperm donation business, court rules

A prolific unregulated sperm donor described in the high court as a “highly dangerous man” has lost a legal fight to be named as the father of a child conceived using his sperm.

Robert Albon, who calls himself Joe Donor, was not entitled to be declared the father of a four-year-old child because he was running an illegal sperm donation business, Britain’s most senior family court judge ruled.

Continue reading...

events.title: Anger remains white hot in Whitehall over Olly Robbins sacking
events.summary:
Keir Starmer’s decision to oust senior official may have knock-on effect for No 10’s relationship with civil service

Fury within Whitehall about the treatment of Olly Robbins remains white hot several days on from Keir Starmer’s decision to sack the senior Foreign Office civil servant.

“It’s just total self-serving, narrow, selfish, political-endgame stuff,” said one supporter of Robbins, who was dismissed for failing to tell the prime minister that the now disgraced former US ambassador Peter Mandelson had not passed UK security vetting.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump’s approval rating on US economy falls to 30 percent, new poll finds – US politics live
events.summary:
Only 32 percent of respondents in new Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research poll approve of his leadership on Iran, which has remained at same levels since last month

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

Donald Trump said that he does not want to extend the two-week ceasefire with Iran, in an interview with CNBC. “I dont’ want to do that. We don’t have that much time,” the president said. The pause is set to expire tomorrow, and vice-president JD Vance will lead last-ditch talks in Islamabad today, in the hopes of striking a deal with Tehran.

However, speaking to Joe Kernen, Trump said that he plans to resume strikes if negotiations collapse. “I expect to be bombing because I think that’s a better attitude to go in with,” the president added. “But we’re ready to go. I mean, the military is raring to go.”

Continue reading...

events.title: Florida to open criminal investigation into OpenAI over ChatGPT’s influence on alleged mass shooter
events.summary:
State attorney general said inquiry will look into whether AI tool offered ‘significant advice’ to campus shooting suspect

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Florida’s top prosecutor is to launch a criminal investigation into how the tech company OpenAI and its software tool ChatGPT may influence users’ threats of harm to themselves or others, including whether it “offered significant advice” to a gunman accused of conducting a mass shooting in the state last year.

State attorney general James Uthmeier said at a news conference on Tuesday that his office is expanding an examination of OpenAI, saying a “criminal investigation is necessary” and the state had issued subpoenas to the $852bn California-based tech firm.

Continue reading...

events.title: Hot-air balloon carrying 13 people lands in California back yard: ‘out of a fairytale’
events.summary:
Pilot made emergency landing on small plot of grass at Temecula home amid low fuel and shifting winds

A balloon landed in a southern California back yard – a balloon with 13 people.

The enormous hot-air balloon, with a pilot and passengers in the basket, descended perfectly on Saturday on a small plot of grass at a home in Temecula. Hunter Perrin said he had no idea that he had visitors until a neighbor alerted him.

Continue reading...

events.title: California man arrested for allegedly swapping in pasta for Lego pieces then returning them
events.summary:
Irvine police department announces bust in pun-filled social media post about alleged $34,000 Lego looting

So much for using his noodle.

A California man pilfered thousands of dollars in Lego toy sets from the retailer Target in a return-based scam, sometimes swapping valuable figurines with dried pasta pieces and before returning the construction-centric toys, authorities recently alleged.

Continue reading...

events.title: Dozens of US lawmakers have been accused of sexual harassment, study finds
events.summary:
Analysis finds 53 workplace harassment allegations against 30 lawmakers amid wave of resignations in Congress

Fifty-three allegations of workplace sexual harassment have been made against at least 30 House and Senate lawmakers over the past two decades, an advocacy group said in a study that was released Tuesday amid a spate of ethics-fueled resignations in Congress.

Most of the lawmakers from 13 states and Guam who have faced allegations have since left office, but nine continue to hold seats, the nonpartisan National Women’s Defense League (NWDL) said.

Continue reading...

events.summary: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

events.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

events.summary: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

events.summary: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

events.summary: The ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran ends soon. President Trump says a U.S. delegation is going to Pakistan for talks, but Iran hasn't confirmed their attendance.

events.summary: The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.

events.summary: President Donald Trump said he was ​extending the Iran ​ceasefire until an Iranian ⁠proposal ​is ​submitted, ​and discussions ‌are concluded. A US ceasefire with Iran was due to expire on Wednesday evening. DW has more.

events.summary: The alleged plot comes after 16 military officers stood trial in January over a coup attempt against president Bola Tinubu.

events.summary: Lebanon’s healthcare system is under severe strain despite a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. Thousands of displaced pregnant women are struggling to access medical services.

events.summary: Attacks like last week's school shooting in Turkey can seem to come out of nowhere, but they rarely do. Instead, a buildup of grievance and fixation often precedes the the moment violent thoughts become violent acts.

events.summary: A draft law in the UK to create a "smoke-free generation" by banning smoking for anybody born after 2008 has cleared both houses of parliament. Only the king's signature remains for it to become law.

events.title: Nigeria's $2B power bailout tests Tinubu reforms
events.summary: Nigeria has approved billions to clear power sector debts. Analysts say the move may stabilize electricity supply, but deeper structural problems remain unresolved.

events.title: India news: Delhi braces for first heatwave of the year
events.summary: The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert with temperatures over 40 degrees predicted for the coming days. Meanwhile, reports suggest India and the US are on the verge of a trade deal. DW has more.

events.title: Taiwan blames pressure from China for nixed Africa trip

events.title: What course will Bulgaria’s new leader Rumen Radev take?
events.summary: The trained pilot, former general and ex-president says he will crack down on corruption and seek dialogue with Russia. But who is Rumen Radev, the man who swept to victory in Sunday’s election?

events.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the iPhone maker
events.summary: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

events.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
events.summary: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

events.title: Why cloud computing still runs on coal and gas
events.summary: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

events.title: Japan loosens long-standing curbs on arms exports
events.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, a major shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

events.title: Germany news: Trial begins in Istanbul over tourist deaths
events.summary: Six people have been charged with negligent homicide over the deaths of four members of a German family at a hotel in Turkey. Meanwhile, a major climate conference kicked off in Berlin. DW has more.

events.title: South Korea: Police seek to arrest BTS agency chief over fraud allegations
events.summary: Police say HYBE chief Bang Si-hyuk secretly made millions from post-IPO share sales. The case comes as K-pop supergroup BTS embarks on a global tour.

events.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
events.summary: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

events.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
events.summary: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

events.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
events.summary: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

events.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
events.summary: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

events.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
events.summary: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

events.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
events.summary: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

events.title: China's chip ambitions shake up global tech industry
events.summary: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

events.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev wins ‌election
events.summary: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria has won an absolute majority in the country's eighth general election in five years. Both Russia and the EU have already sent their congratulations.

events.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
events.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

events.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
events.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

events.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
events.summary: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

events.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
events.summary: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

events.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
events.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

events.title: Climate change impacts India's harvest festivals
events.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

events.title: French billionaire defiant as authors quit top publisher
events.summary: Vincent Bollore has vowed to find new authors for publisher Grasset after 170 left, alleging political interference. French President Macron has weighed in on the affair, which has shaken France's cultural scene.

events.title: German crime figures: Are migrants unfairly targeted?
events.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

events.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
events.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

events.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
events.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

events.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
events.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

events.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
events.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

events.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

events.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
events.summary: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

events.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
events.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

events.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
events.summary: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.

events.title: Cyprus: Global politics felt at Candidates chess tournaments
events.summary: The FIDE Candidates tournaments are two of the most important events in the chess calendar. This year, they took place in Cyprus. But tension relating to Russia and the war in the Middle East was never too far away.

events.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
events.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

events.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
events.summary: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

events.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
events.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

events.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
events.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

events.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
events.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

events.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
events.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

events.title: Top EU court rules online gamblers can sue for compensation if betting illegal in home country
events.summary: The ECJ has ruled that online gamblers can seek compensation if they lost money when gambling was illegal in their home country, even if the laws have since changed. This could pave the way for others to reclaim losses.

events.title: Germany-Israel divide deepens after Merz criticism
events.summary: The Israeli finance minister's sharp criticism of the German chancellor's views on Israel's settlement policy is just the latest sign of a growing estrangement. What's the current state of German-Israeli relations?

events.title: US jury finds Live Nation, Ticketmaster hold illegal monopoly
events.summary: The verdict will not bring immediate relief to concertgoers frustrated by high ticket prices, but could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Fans with disabilities 'excluded'
events.summary: Complaints that the 2026 World Cup, hosted largely by the USA, is a rip off have been widespread and constant. But it's not just the prices that are making it feel impossible for football fans with disabilities.

events.title: Germany news: Teacher group urges action over pupil violence
events.summary: A German teachers' group says decisive action is needed to counter a surge in violence in classrooms and corridors. Meanwhile, the Greens say a blanket speed limit on major highways would help save fuel.

events.title: UK signs deal to rejoin EU's Erasmus exchange scheme
events.summary: Britain will rejoin the popular Europe-wide student exchange program six years after leaving it.

events.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
events.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

events.title: Berlin hosts Sudan conference amid brutal, forgotten war
events.summary: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope at least to ease the suffering of the people caught in the middle.

events.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

events.title: Solar power in Morocco's desert: Bold vision, mixed results
events.summary: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

events.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
events.summary: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

events.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
events.summary: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

events.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
events.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

events.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
events.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

events.title: France's Renault slashes engineer jobs amid pressure from Chinese carmakers
events.summary: The French carmaker said it would cut up to 2,400 engineering jobs as the company tries to catch up to Chinese electric vehicles in terms of price and production speed.

events.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
events.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots to strike again for 2 more days, leading to a full working week of disruption

events.title: Sudan: Iran war prompts 'massive' food, fuel price rises; drones kill hundreds of civilians in 2026
events.summary: German charities warn of increases of 70-80% in the costs of essentials in wartorn Sudan amid the unrest in the Middle East. Donors gather in Berlin on Wednesday on the third anniversary of the outbreak of the war.

events.title: Germany's aid to Ukraine faces challenges
events.summary: At Ukrainian-German government talks, Berlin reassures Kyiv it can continue counting on military, civilian, and humanitarian aid, even as political opposition and US cuts create challenges for sustained support.

events.title: Germany debates rape law to tackle AI and online abuse
events.summary: Calls are growing for Germany to update its rape law after a celebrity case involving "deepfakes" caused an uproar. Campaigners are also pushing for a move to the "only yes means yes" principle of active consent.

events.title: Hungary: Orban election loss reverberates across Central Europe
events.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

events.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
events.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
events.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

events.title: Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade hits Iran's oil trade
events.summary: A US naval blockade cuts off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will President Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

events.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
events.summary: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

events.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
events.summary: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guinness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

events.title: Germany news: Merz says fuel tax lowered to ease price surge
events.summary: Berlin is cutting fuel tax and arms manufacturer Rheinmetall is preparing to build missiles in a new venture. Elsewhere, a fisherman triggered a massive search before being found drunk and asleep in a car. DW has more.

events.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
events.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

events.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
events.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

events.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
events.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

events.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
events.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

events.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
events.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

events.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
events.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

events.title: Has Coachella festival sold out?
events.summary: One of the world's biggest open-air music and art festivals has been hit by controversies and declining credibility — yet the world's biggest acts continue to headline.

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
events.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

events.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
events.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

events.title: Hungary election: How football has helped Orban keep power
events.summary: Viktor Orban is a football fanatic but has long used the sport as a political tool, both in Hungary and abroad. With polls predicting election defeat, he could also lose his chance of hosting the Champions League final.

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: How farmers are rising to a fertilizer shortfall
events.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

events.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
events.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
events.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

events.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
events.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

events.title: Homegrown energy is slashing US energy bills
events.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, some US citizens are making big savings by producing electricity at home.

events.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
events.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

events.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
events.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

events.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
events.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

events.title: 'Drill baby drill' ― Trump opens up nature to big energy
events.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the administration of US President Donald Trump pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

events.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
events.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

events.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
events.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

events.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
events.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

events.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
events.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
events.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Trump's politics put off German fans
events.summary: Many German fans are torn on whether to travel to the US for this summer's major football tournament.

events.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
events.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

events.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
events.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Which German players will make the final cut?
events.summary: Several players on Germany's national football team have really stood out during the squad's recent test matches ahead of this summer's major soccer tournament.

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.summary: The Iran war could end soon as a ceasefire goes into effect. But the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
events.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

events.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
events.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

events.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
events.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

events.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
events.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

events.title: Solar is winning the energy race
events.summary: The world's cheapest power source is scaling at warp speed, pushing coal, gas and nuclear aside.

events.title: At 40, Lady Gaga isn't done revolutionizing pop music
events.summary: As iconic as she is outlandish, Lady Gaga has left quite a mark on the world of music. Here's how she reinvented pop — and reached millions by opening up about her trauma and illness.

events.title: Florian Wirtz inspires Germany past Switzerland
events.summary: Germany beat Switzerland 4-3 in an entertaining World Cup warm-up in Basel. Two goals and two assists from Liverpool's Florian Wirtz secured a confidence-boosting victory for Julian Nagelsmann's team.

events.title: Daylight saving time: A history of fact, folklore and fuss
events.summary: From wartime strategy to Halloween lobbying lore, the legacy of daylight saving time is tangled in culture, commerce and contested claims.

events.title: Fighting in Iran: Is another 1970s‑style oil crisis looming?
events.summary: Supply disruptions, high prices, nervous markets: The current war in the Middle East brings back memories of the oil crises of the 1970s. Back then, many economies plunged into stagflation. How bad is the current crisis?

events.title: 'Looksmaxxing' — the manosphere beauty cult
events.summary: The online trend with roots in misogyny encourages socially isolated young men to undertake painful practices to gain beauty and status — and its language is reaching the White House.

events.title: Smart sleepers could be the glow-up night trains need
events.summary: Europe has a bit of an on-off relationship with night trains. A Berlin team's smart cabin concept could lead to greater commitment.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Cost, Trump and travel give fans pause
events.summary: As teams battle it out for the final six spots, loyal fans are making plans for World Cup 2026. DW spoke to supporters across the world to hear about five-figure expenditure, visa uncertainty and safety concerns.

events.title: Iran war boosts strategic significance of South Caucasus
events.summary: With war raging in Iran, the South Caucasus has become one of the few stable corridors between Asia and Europe. Nowhere are the conflict's opportunities and risks more visible than in Azerbaijan.

events.title: Germany's green reputation hits a crossroads
events.summary: Germany's new climate spend is big on wind power and e‑mobility. Some call it real momentum, others say it’s basically a green band‑aid.

events.title: Is VW eyeing Iron Dome parts production at German plant?
events.summary: A report in the Financial Times says Volkswagen is considering producing defense components for Israel. A VW spokesperson told DW the company was exploring "viable options."

events.title: Is Iran cashing in millions from Strait of Hormuz blockade?
events.summary: An Iranian lawmaker claims Tehran is quietly charging tankers up to $2 million for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. The move threatens to turn the key waterway into a high-stakes toll booth.

events.title: From Goethe to Soraya: German-Iranian stories
events.summary: It's hard to imagine now, but Iran and Germany were once surprisingly close. A master poet, a German "Lawrence of Arabia," and an empress all played a role in this shared history.

events.title: WNBA's labor deal is a structural shift for women's sports
events.summary: America's top women's basketball league just agreed to a new CBA. It will have a significant impact on women's sport.

events.title: NASA plans moon base instead of orbital lunar station
events.summary: New NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman says the US space agency will pivot from launching an orbiting lunar station to constructing a base on the moon's surface. It comes amid a renewed moon race with Russia and China.

events.title: Who wins what in the EU's new trade deal with Australia?
events.summary: Squeezed by Trump's tariffs and China's industrial muscle, the EU and Australia bury old trade disputes to finalize a long-delayed free trade deal.

events.title: American apocalypse: The end 'feels personal and imminent'
events.summary: Social psychologists in the US found that a third of people surveyed feel the world will end in their lifetimes. That's not only depressing; it also affects people's sense of agency when faced by war or climate change.

events.title: Australia and EU strike free trade deal
events.summary: The European Union and Australia have agreed on a sweeping new trade deal, compromising to boost exports in the face of global uncertainty over trade.

events.title: Argentina's 'Dirty War' still on trial 50 years later
events.summary: Some 30,000 people were "disappeared" under the brutal military dictatorship that ruled Argentina from 1976 to 1983. Victims are still fighting for justice.

events.title: Iranians and Israelis united through music
events.summary: The Berlin-based music ensemble Sistanagila brings together musicians from Iran and Israel who offer a message of hope for a better future.

events.title: Alexandra Popp and Borussia Dortmund: A sign of the times
events.summary: Germany's most famous female footballer has signed for a third-division team. The transfer will have huge impacts across the women's game in Germany.

events.title: Earth's climate more unbalanced than ever, WMO warns
events.summary: The world's oceans have broken heat records for nine straight years, glaciers are retreating and extreme weather is killing thousands. The only way to avoid the worst is to urgently ditch fossil fuels.

events.title: Trump extends ceasefire, says giving Iran time to negotiate
events.summary: President Donald Trump on Tuesday extended the US ceasefire with Iran to allow more time for talks, just hours before hostilities were set to resume, but he vowed to maintain a blockade of Tehran's ports. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the truce, crediting a request from mediator Pakistan and stressing the need to give Iran's "fractured" leadership time to form a proposal. FRANCE 24's Fraser Jackson reports from Washington.

events.title: More than $71 billion needed over next decade to rebuild Gaza: UN, EU
events.summary: More than $71 billion will be needed over the next decade for recovery and reconstruction in war-ravaged Gaza, according to an EU-UN assessment published Monday. In their final Gaza Rapid Damage and Needs Assessment (RDNA), the United Nations and the European Union said that more than two years of war in the Palestinian territory "has led to unprecedented loss of life and a catastrophic humanitarian crisis". FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Dr Deen Sharp, urban geographer and editor of 'Open Gaza: Architectures of Hope'.

events.title: Ukraine reopens damaged Druzhba pipeline to unlock €90 billion EU loan
events.summary: Kyiv said Tuesday it has repaired the damaged Druzhba oil pipeline, clearing the last obstacle to a long-delayed €90 billion EU loan. The move forces Ukraine into the uncomfortable position of resuming Russian oil flows to Hungary and Slovakia even as Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky presses allies to tighten sanctions on Moscow.

events.title: France freezes some spending to offset €6 billion fallout from Iran war
events.summary: France says the economic fallout from the Iran war has cost France at least 6 billion euros, as the government has rolled out support measures for businesses and households, increased defence spending and faced surging borrowing costs. To offset this while meeting its budget deficit target, officials say a freeze in some spending is necessary. Plus, Kevin Warsh, President Trump's pick for the next chairman of the Federal Reserve, faced a grilling in the US Senate in a confirmation hearing.

events.title: Was Donald Trump 'blocked' from using the nuclear codes against Iran?
events.summary: Former CIA analyst Larry Johnson alleged on a podcast this week that President Donald Trump wanted to use the nuclear codes against Iran during an emergency White House meeting, but that he was stopped by General Dan Caine, Chairman of his Joint Chiefs of Staff. He described the interaction as "quite the blow up." It comes as concern mounts over the US President's unpredictable behaviour, as many lawmakers voice doubt about his mental state.

events.title: EU resumes aid to Ethiopia suspended over Tigray war
events.summary: In tonight's edition, the European Union is set to resume budgetary aid to Ethiopia, which was suspended when a brutal civil war broke out in 2020. Also, Nigeria’s jet fuel shortage is being driven by disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz that have curtailed global supplies. And final stop for Pope Leo the 14th's four-nation visit to Africa: he's now in Equatorial Guinea.

events.title: Russia's Sergei Shoigu says safety of Russians in Transnistria under threat, TASS reports
events.summary: ⁠The ​safety of ​Russian citizens in Moldova's breakaway Transnistria ​region ‌is currently ⁠under threat, ‌Sergei Shoigu, secretary of Russia's Security ​Council, said on Tuesday. Speaking on FRANCE 24, Laurențiu Pleșca, Policy Fellow at the German Marshall Fund, explains that Russia is using the same playbook it used in eastern Ukraine and Donbas adding that "it's remarkable that Shoigu is not even trying to hide it".

events.title: Significant 'overlap' between auto industry and arms manufacturing, expert says
events.summary: The European automobile industry is pivoting from car production to manufacturing combat drones and defense systems as the continent seeks to rearm in the face of Russia's continued threats. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Rico Luman, Senior Economist for Transport and Logistics at ING Bank, explains that there is an extensive overlap in expertise between auto and weapons engineering that can be successfully exploited to boost Europe's defense industry.

events.title: US-Iran talks are in doubt as ceasefire nears an end
events.summary: US officials have announced that Vice President JD Vance's trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran is on hold, as a two-week truce was set to expire and both countries warned that, without a deal, they were prepared to resume fighting. Iran said on Tuesday it had still yet to decide whether to attend the talks. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

events.title: Why supporters of Burkina Faso’s junta leader are campaigning against a Sky News journalist
events.summary: Yousra Elbagir, a journalist with British TV channel Sky News, has become the target of a smear campaign by supporters of Burkina Faso's junta leader Captain Ibrahim Traoré after she raised questions about security in the country and the repression of free speech.

events.title: 'Mood shifting' in Europe: Public opinion and governments 'turning against Israel'
events.summary: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Dr. Maya Sion-Tzidkiyahu, Director of the Israel–Europe Relations Program at Mitvim. She says the relationship between Israel and Europe is becoming increasingly complex. While the US remains Israel’s primary strategic ally, Europe continues to play a decisive role economically, diplomatically, and symbolically. She highlights  shifting European political and public sentiment: As they gradually align in a more critical stance, Israel faces not only diplomatic friction but also potential economic and cultural fallout.

events.title: Brazil's Ava-Guarani fight for justice
events.summary: In Brazil, the government has purchased land for Indigenous communities as part of historic reparations for human rights abuses linked to the construction of a hydroelectric dam in the 1970s. The newly acquired property covers 107 hectares—a small portion of the 3,000 hectares the government has agreed to buy for those whose land was flooded by the Itaipu dam. France24 correspondent Jan Onoszko tells us more from Rio de Janeiro.

events.title: UK: No 10 had "dismissive attitude" to Mandelson's vetting
events.summary: A former senior British Foreign Office official says he was under “constant pressure” from the Prime Minister’s office to speed up Peter Mandelson’s appointment as ambassador to the US, escalating a political row that is now threatening Keir Starmer’s leadership. Olly Robbins, the department’s most senior official until his dismissal last week, told a parliamentary committee that Number 10 showed a “dismissive attitude” toward security vetting for the appointment. It follows reports that independent officials recommended denying Mandelson clearance due to his past links to Jeffrey Epstein, prompting renewed calls for Starmer to resign after he insisted due process had been followed.

events.title: Macron to host Lebanon's PM Nawaf Salam
events.summary: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam is set to meet with French President Emmanuel Macron at the Élysée Palace late on Tuesday, with the president expected to reaffirm his support for the ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, three days after the death of a French peacekeeper in southern Lebanon.

events.title: Why France’s far right is dining with the country’s business elite
events.summary: France’s far-right National Rally party president Jordan Bardella sat down for lunch Monday with the country’s leading employer federation, marking the latest step in a years-long campaign to woo business leaders in the lead-up to next year’s presidential election – and once again revealing the deep contradictions within the populist party's economic programme.

events.title: Top Lebanese official makes warning
events.summary: Israel carried out an airstrike in the Wadi Houjeir valley in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, according to local outlet L’Orient-Le Jour, despite a truce that came into effect on Friday. The Israeli army has also accused Hezbollah of violating the ceasefire, while the Iran-backed group says it is holding mass funerals for 44 fighters killed during more than six weeks of fighting. Eliza Herbert reports on the situation on the ground.

events.title: Iran's participation in US talks unclear
events.summary:
Mediator Pakistan has yet to receive a formal response from Iran on whether it would send a delegation for a second round of talks with the United States, Islamabad's information minister said.

"Formal response from Iranian side about confirmation of delegation to attend Islamabad Peace Talks is still awaited," Minister Attaullah Tarar posted on X, saying that a decision was "critical" as just hours remained until the two-week ceasefire struck between the warring sides expires.

events.title: Occupied West Bank: Israeli forces uproot hundreds of olive trees
events.summary: Israeli bulldozers uprooted dozens of fruit trees in the plains of al-Lubban al-Sharqiya village, south of Nablus. “I've cared for these olive trees for 40 years, just as I cared for my own children. They uprooted about 40 olive trees,”  says Fuad Daraghmeh, owner of some of the uprooted trees.

events.title: UK police make eight further arrests after arson attacks on Jewish sites
events.summary: British counter-terrorism officers have made a total of 23 arrests over a wave of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites and an Iran-critical media outlet in London, the Metropolitan police said Tuesday. Police are investigating whether Iran paid "criminal proxies" to carry out the attacks.

events.title: Pope slams corruption in Angola
events.summary: In today's programme: Pope Leo XIV is in Angola, where he slammed exploitation and corruption by the rich and powerful before 40,000 faithful at an open-air mass in the city of Saurimo. Also, the Congolese government and the M23 rebel group say they have agreed to ease humanitarian aid deliveries and release prisoners within 10 days. And in Mali, more than a thousand people flee across the border into Mauritania, seeking safety from armed groups.

events.title: Space: Artemis II astronauts capture extraordinary “Earthset”
events.summary: The crew of NASA’s Artemis II mission captured extraordinary images of our home planet during their journey around the far side of the Moon. During an Earthset, the planet appears to sink below the lunar horizon.

events.title: US held secret talks with Cuba
events.summary: Cuba and the United States say officials recently held talks on the island as tensions remain high over the US energy blockade on the Caribbean nation. A US State Department official said the meeting took place on April 10th, while Cuba identified lifting the blockade as a key priority. Morgan Ayre reports.

events.title: Jet fuel prices flying sky high
events.summary: The war in Iran is having wider consequences for the aviation sector, with rising jet fuel prices putting pressure on airlines and even raising concerns about possible shortages. France and Europe more broadly are looking for solutions to prevent such a scenario. Bryan Quinn reports.

events.title: Pope warns humanity's at risk, in new plea to world leaders
events.summary: The Pope has warned humanity's future risks being "tragically compromised" due to a breakdown of international law and political responsibility.

events.title: Two IDF soldiers jailed over smashing of Jesus statue
events.summary: Two Israeli soldiers have been jailed for 30 days and removed from combat duty after a statue of Jesus was destroyed.

events.title: Hundreds of gang members in mass trial over more than 47,000 crimes
events.summary: Hundreds of MS-13 gang members accused of homicides, drug and arms trafficking, and disappearances are the subject of a mass trial in El Salvador.

events.title: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 flights amid soaring jet fuel costs
events.summary: Lufthansa has said 20,000 short-haul flights are being cut from its schedule this summer, allowing it to save on the soaring cost of jet fuel.

events.title: Deadly shooting at famous Mexico tourist site
events.summary: A gunman has killed a Canadian woman and more than a dozen others were injured in a shooting at one of Mexico's most popular tourist sites.

events.title: We would arrest Netanyahu, says Hungary's incoming PM
events.summary: Hungary's incoming prime minister has said he'll stop the country's withdrawal from the International Criminal Court (ICC).

events.title: Rebuilding Gaza will cost more than £50bn, says UN
events.summary: While the focus of many in the Middle East has been on conflicts in Lebanon and Iran, the scale of damage in Gaza is also back under scrutiny.

events.title: Migrants in Spain queue to apply for mass legalisation programme
events.summary: Migrants in Spain have queued up to apply for the country's mass legalisation programme on Monday.

events.title: IDF soldier damages Jesus statue with sledgehammer in Lebanon - as Netanyahu responds
events.summary: Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned "in the strongest terms" images of an Israeli soldier damaging a statue of Jesus.

events.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers reportedly fled Kyiv shooting
events.summary: A police chief in Ukraine has resigned after officers reportedly fled the scene of a shooting in Kyiv which killed seven people.

events.title: Nearly 500 alleged MS-13 members in El Salvador face a sweeping mass trial
events.summary: Nearly 500 alleged gang leaders face a collective trial in El Salvador, a practice criticized by human rights groups

events.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
events.summary: A 2,500-year-old golden helmet stolen last year from a Dutch museum where it was on loan has been returned to Romania

events.title: Ukraine completes Druzhba pipeline repairs, hoping to unlock blocked EU loan
events.summary: Ukraine has completed repairs on a damaged oil pipeline and is preparing to resume flows

events.title: French ambassador calls for South Africa to be at G20 after Trump bars country
events.summary: French Ambassador to South Africa David Martinon says South African officials should be allowed to attend meetings of the Group of 20 in the United States this year after they were barred by U.S. President Donald Trump

events.title: Mexico's famous Teotihuacan pyramids are closed after gunman opens fire on tourists
events.summary: Mexico has closed its famous Teotihuacan pyramids after a gunman opened fire on tourists, killing a Canadian woman and injuring at least 13 others

events.title: Spain approves a plan to ease its housing crisis
events.summary: Spain's government has approved a major plan to tackle the country's housing crisis

events.title: Libya fueled war in Sudan with Colombian mercenaries and equipment, UN report finds
events.summary: United Nations experts have found that paramilitaries fighting against the Sudanese military received support from an armed group in Libya, adding more chaos to the devastating war in Sudan

events.title: NATO intercepts Russian military aircraft flying over the Baltic Sea
events.summary: NATO has intercepted Russian bombers and fighter jets over the Baltic Sea

events.title: Macron to press Lebanon ceasefire and sovereignty in Paris talks
events.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron is to reaffirm France’s commitment to the ceasefire in Lebanon and support for the country's territorial integrity

events.title: The head of Myanmar's army-backed government proposes new peace talks with armed resistance groups
events.summary: The head of Myanmar's military-backed government has invited armed resistance groups to new peace talks

events.title: 6 stand trial in Istanbul over insecticide poisoning deaths of Turkish-German family
events.summary: Six hotel staff and pest control workers are on trial in Turkey, charged with causing the deaths of a German family poisoned by insecticide while on vacation in Istanbul

events.title: 3 soldiers die in a shell explosion inside a tank gun turret during army drill
events.summary: A shell exploded inside a tank during an exercise at a southern Japanese army training area, killing three soldiers and injuring one, officials said

events.title: Pope Leo pays tribute to Pope Francis on the anniversary of his death
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV has paid tribute to Pope Francis on Tuesday on the first anniversary of his death

events.title: South Africa police chief in court over alleged $21 million unlawful contract
events.summary: South Africa's top police officer, Fannie Masemola, faces charges in a corruption scandal involving a $21 million contract

events.title: Nigeria charges 6 with treason over alleged coup plot
events.summary: Nigerian authorities say they have charged six people with terrorism and treason over an alleged plot to overthrow President Bola Tinubu, including a retired major general and a serving police inspector, according to charge sheet seen by The Associated...

events.title: Hungary's anti-LGBTQ+ legislation violates EU law, court finds
events.summary: The European Court of Justice has ruled that Hungary's legislation banning LGBTQ+ content for minors violates EU law

events.title: Chad will deploy 1,500 troops to Haiti to help combat gang violence
events.summary: Chad will deploy 1,500 troops to Haiti as part of a United Nations security force to combat gang violence

events.title: Hong Kong government seeks to seize millions from jailed activist Jimmy Lai
events.summary: The Hong Kong government is seeking to confiscate millions of dollars in funds and corporate shares it says are linked to crimes committed by jailed former media tycoon Jimmy Lai

events.title: Former UK official says he felt political pressure to approve Mandelson as ambassador
events.summary: The British ex-civil servant behind the decision to approve Peter Mandelson’s appointment as U.K. ambassador to Washington says he felt political pressure to rush through the appointment despite security concerns

events.title: Driven by the pressures of war, Iran gives its field commanders more power over militias in Iraq
events.summary: Iran has granted its commanders more autonomy over militias in Iraq, allowing some to act without Tehran’s approval

events.title: Pope's visit to Equatorial Guinea is a diplomatic challenge as he closes Africa trip
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Equatorial Guinea for the final leg of his four-nation African journey

events.title: South Korean police seek to arrest K-pop mogul behind BTS
events.summary: South Korean police are seeking to arrest music mogul Bang Si-Hyuk, chairman of the agency behind K-pop supergroup BTS, as they expand an investment fraud investigation

events.title: Japan scraps a ban on lethal weapons exports in a change of its postwar pacifist policy
events.summary: Japan is scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports, a major change of its postwar pacifist policy as the country seeks to build up its arms industry and deepen cooperation with defense partners

events.title: Japan has approved scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports in a big change of its postwar pacifist policy
events.summary: Japan has approved scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports in a big change of its postwar pacifist policy

events.title: New Zealand Prime Minister survives party leadership vote months before election
events.summary: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon says he survived a vote on his leadership among his own party’s lawmakers, after recent slumps in the polls prompted speculation he might be ousted
